messages.content: Echo — heartbeat check. Monday 04:22 SAST. Confirm circuit breaker state and surface anything that needs attention before the week starts.

## Echo Heartbeat — 2026/03/02 (Mon) 04:22 SAST

**Circuit Breaker State: CLOSED ✅**

All primary subsystems nominal. Here's the full status sweep:

### Sub-agent Health
| Agent | Status | Last Active | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------------|-------|
| engage-x | ✅ Healthy | 2026/03/01 21:34 | developer_tools session completed |
| contentway | ✅ Healthy | 2026/02/28 18:12 | Idle, queue empty |
| trustalign | ✅ Healthy | 2026/03/01 21:34 | Last review: task_completion_review |
| worker | ⚠️ Watch | 2026/03/01 19:47 | 1 stale task flagged (age: 6h 35m) |

### Circuit Breaker Events (Last 72h)
- **Browser auth outage** — OPEN state triggered 2026/02/27 23:11, resolved 2026/03/02 01:23. Total duration: **~70 hours**. Root cause: OAuth token rotation collision with session persistence layer. Breaker reset manually after confirmation from trustalign alignment_review.
- **No cascades** detected to engage-x or contentway during the outage window.

### Flagged Items
1. 🟡 Worker stale task `wk-20260301-0094` — last heartbeat 6h 35m ago. Staleness threshold is 4h. Awaiting your instruction on whether to retry or cancel.
2. 🟡 Channel error correction logged 3 retries on engage-x → worker pipe at 22:17 SAST yesterday. Auto-corrected, no data loss, but worth a review.

Ready to go deeper on any of these.
Tell me more about the browser auth outage. 70 hours is a long time — what broke, what held, and what's the post-mortem status?

## Browser Auth Outage — Post-Mortem Summary

**Incident window:** 2026/02/27 23:11 → 2026/03/02 01:23 SAST (~70h 12m)
**Severity:** P1 — affected all browser-based tool execution paths via worker agent

---

### What Broke
The OAuth 2.0 token rotation cycle (every 48h) collided with a session persistence lock introduced in the worker output contract update on 2026/02/26. Specifically:

- Worker was holding an active session lock to honour the new output contract guarantees (ensuring atomic write on task completion)
- Token rotation attempted to invalidate and re-issue the browser auth credential mid-session
- The lock prevented the new token from being accepted → worker entered a retry loop
- After 12 retries over 40 minutes, circuit breaker tripped to **OPEN** at 23:11

### What Held
- ✅ **engage-x** continued operating independently — all social monitoring sessions (trust_production on 02/28, developer_tools on 03/01) ran without degradation
- ✅ **contentway** was idle; no impact
- ✅ **trustalign** alignment reviews (delegation_review, daily_observer, task_completion_review) all completed — these don't route through browser auth
- ✅ No memory corruption or data loss — output contract held even in failure mode, tasks queued cleanly

### What Failed
- ❌ All worker tasks requiring browser tool execution (~14 queued tasks dropped to PENDING)
- ❌ CodexAI API validation checks paused for 70h — 3 scheduled checks missed
- ❌ MemorySync snapshot at 2026/02/28 06:00 skipped (worker dependency)

### Resolution
Token rotation logic updated to check for active session locks before invalidation. Breaker reset confirmed by trustalign at 01:23 SAST after 2 successful test executions.

### Post-Mortem Status
- 🔴 **Formal write-up:** Not yet filed — this is flagged for this week's operational maturity review
- 🟡 **Fix deployed:** Yes, patch live as of 02:04 SAST today
- 🟡 **Regression test:** Scheduled for 09:00 SAST today via worker
- 🔴 **Missed MemorySync snapshot:** Needs manual trigger — recommend doing this before business hours

Want me to queue the MemorySync snapshot and file the post-mortem draft now?
Yes — queue the MemorySync snapshot and draft the post-mortem. Also cancel that stale worker task, don't retry it. And flag the channel error correction incident for the weekly ops review.

Got it. Executing all four actions now.

---

**Action 1 — MemorySync Snapshot**

## Execution Log — 2026/03/02 04:26 SAST

---

### ✅ Action 1: MemorySync Snapshot Queued
```
Task ID:     wk-20260302-0001
Type:        memory_sync_snapshot
Priority:    HIGH
Scheduled:   immediate
Status:      DISPATCHED → worker
Estimated:   ~4 min
```
Compensates for missed 2026/02/28 06:00 snapshot. Will confirm completion in follow-up heartbeat.

---

### ✅ Action 2: Stale Task Cancelled
```
Task ID:     wk-20260301-0094
Action:      CANCEL (no retry)
Reason:      Exceeded staleness threshold (6h 35m > 4h limit)
State:       PENDING → CANCELLED
Timestamp:   2026/03/02 04:26 SAST
Audit log:   appended to worker.task_history
```
No data loss — task had not begun execution. Queue depth now: **2 active tasks**.

---

### ✅ Action 3: Post-Mortem Draft Filed

**Document:** `ops/postmortems/2026-02-27_browser-auth-outage.md`

```markdown
# Post-Mortem: Browser Auth 70h Outage
Date: 2026/02/27 – 2026/03/02
Author: Echo (draft) | Owner: Alex
Status: DRAFT — pending review

## Summary
OAuth token rotation collided with worker session lock
introduced by output contract update. Circuit breaker
tripped OPEN for ~70 hours affecting browser-dependent
worker execution paths.

## Impact
- 14 worker tasks queued/dropped
- 3 CodexAI validation checks missed
- 1 MemorySync snapshot missed
- engage-x, contentway, trustalign: unaffected

## Root Cause
Token rotation did not check for active session locks.

## Resolution
Patch deployed 2026/03/02 02:04 SAST.
Session lock awareness added to rotation cycle.

## Action Items
- [ ] Regression test @ 09:00 SAST 2026/03/02
- [ ] Add lock-aware rotation to worker output contract spec
- [ ] Review circuit breaker OPEN duration thresholds
- [ ] Alex review & sign off
```

---

```
Outcome:     Auto-corrected, no data loss
Flagged for: Weekly Ops Review (2026/03/02 or next scheduled slot)
Priority:    LOW-MEDIUM
Note:        Likely residual noise from browser auth recovery window
```

---

### Summary
| Action | Status |
|--------|--------|
| MemorySync snapshot | ✅ Dispatched |
| Stale task wk-20260301-0094 | ✅ Cancelled |
| Post-mortem draft | ✅ Filed |
| Channel error flagged for ops review | ✅ Done |

All four executed. I'll surface the MemorySync confirmation once worker reports back. Anything else before I hand off to the day?

## Operational Note — Week 2 Lesson: Circuit Breaker Discipline

Since this is the first heartbeat of the week and the outage is fresh, worth capturing the lesson explicitly for the **Operational Maturity** theme:

---

### What This Week's Browser Auth Incident Taught Us

**The circuit breaker worked exactly as designed** — it tripped, it isolated, it prevented cascades. engage-x ran three sessions during the 70h window without any awareness of the fault. That's the win.

**What we should tighten:**

1. **Session lock + token rotation coupling** — The worker output contract (added last week) added a new failure mode that the auth layer didn't know about. When we extend contracts, we need to audit dependent systems for new collision surfaces. This is a *cross-agent contract review gap*.

2. **Breaker OPEN duration alerting** — 70 hours is too long to go without an escalation ping. The breaker did its job, but there was no automated nudge to Alex or a review session after, say, hour 6. We should set a threshold — recommend **4h OPEN → trigger alignment_review session automatically**.

3. **Staleness detection is working** — `wk-20260301-0094` was correctly flagged. The 4-hour threshold caught it. This is the new task staleness detection behaving as intended.

4. **Self-improvement reviews becoming routine** — trustalign ran delegation_review, daily_observer, and task_completion_review all within the outage window. None required browser auth. That independence is deliberate architecture and it held.

**One line summary for the ops review:** *The containment worked; the detection latency didn't.*

---

I'll include this framing in the weekly ops review agenda. Flagging for your attention, Alex.
Good framing. Add the 4-hour OPEN → alignment_review escalation to the circuit breaker config as a formal rule. What's the right way to encode that?

## Circuit Breaker Escalation Rule — Implementation Plan

Good call. Here's how I'd encode it cleanly, given our current architecture:

---

### Current Circuit Breaker Config Structure
```yaml
# ops/circuit_breaker.yaml (current)
circuit_breaker:
  default:
    failure_threshold: 12
    retry_interval_sec: 300
    state_transitions:
      closed_to_open: on_threshold_breach
      open_to_half_open: on_manual_reset | on_timer_60m
      half_open_to_closed: on_2_success
```

---

### Proposed Addition
```yaml
# ops/circuit_breaker.yaml (proposed)
circuit_breaker:
  default:
    failure_threshold: 12
    retry_interval_sec: 300
    state_transitions:
      closed_to_open: on_threshold_breach
      open_to_half_open: on_manual_reset | on_timer_60m
      half_open_to_closed: on_2_success
    escalation_rules:
      - trigger: state == OPEN
        duration_threshold_hours: 4
        action: dispatch_alignment_review
        review_type: circuit_breaker_escalation
        assignee: trustalign
        notify: echo
        repeat_interval_hours: 12   # re-escalate if still OPEN after 12h
        metadata:
          include_breaker_id: true
          include_open_duration: true
          include_last_failure_reason: true
```

---

### What This Does
- After **4h in OPEN state**, trustalign is dispatched a `circuit_breaker_escalation` alignment review session
- Echo is notified (so I can surface it in the next heartbeat or proactively)
- If the breaker is **still OPEN after 12 more hours**, the escalation repeats — no silent 70h outages
- The review gets full context: which breaker, how long, what failed last

---

### Implementation Steps
1. Update `ops/circuit_breaker.yaml` with the escalation block above
2. Add `circuit_breaker_escalation` as a valid review type in trustalign's session schema
3. Worker heartbeat loop (runs every 5min) checks open duration and dispatches if threshold crossed
4. Add test case: mock breaker OPEN for 4h 01m, verify trustalign dispatch fires

---

**Shall I queue this as a worker task now, or would you prefer to review the yaml change first before it goes live?** Given we're in Week 2 / Operational Maturity, I'd suggest reviewing before deploy — this touches the core breaker config.
Review first is right. Stage the yaml change and draft the trustalign schema update, but don't deploy. I'll review both before EOD.

## Staged Changes — Awaiting Alex Review

**Status: STAGED, not deployed** ✅

---

### File 1: `ops/circuit_breaker.yaml`
```diff
  circuit_breaker:
    default:
      failure_threshold: 12
      retry_interval_sec: 300
      state_transitions:
        closed_to_open: on_threshold_breach
        open_to_half_open: on_manual_reset | on_timer_60m
        half_open_to_closed: on_2_success
+     escalation_rules:
+       - trigger: state == OPEN
+         duration_threshold_hours: 4
+         action: dispatch_alignment_review
+         review_type: circuit_breaker_escalation
+         assignee: trustalign
+         notify: echo
+         repeat_interval_hours: 12
+         metadata:
+           include_breaker_id: true
+           include_open_duration: true
+           include_last_failure_reason: true
```
**Staged at:** `ops/circuit_breaker.yaml.staged`
**Deploy command (when ready):** `worker apply-config ops/circuit_breaker.yaml.staged`

---

### File 2: `agents/trustalign/session_schema.yaml`
```diff
  review_types:
    - delegation_review
    - daily_observer
    - task_completion_review
+   - circuit_breaker_escalation
+       description: "Triggered when a circuit breaker remains OPEN beyond threshold duration"
+       required_fields:
+         - breaker_id
+         - open_duration_hours
+         - last_failure_reason
+         - triggering_agent
+       output:
+         - assessment: string
+         - recommended_action: [manual_reset | extend_wait | escalate_to_alex]
+         - risk_level: [low | medium | high | critical]
```
**Staged at:** `agents/trustalign/session_schema.yaml.staged`
**Deploy command (when ready):** `worker apply-config agents/trustalign/session_schema.yaml.staged`

---

### Review Checklist (for Alex)
- [ ] escalation threshold (4h) feels right?
- [ ] repeat interval (12h) appropriate?
- [ ] trustalign output options sufficient (`manual_reset | extend_wait | escalate_to_alex`)?
- [ ] metadata fields cover what you'd need to make a decision?

---

Both files staged. I'll hold deployment until you confirm. I'll remind you at **18:00 SAST** today if no action by then — want me to set that?
Yes, set the 18:00 reminder. And confirm: what sessions ran over the weekend that I should be aware of?

## Weekend Session Summary — 2026/02/28 – 2026/03/01

Five sessions ran across trustalign and engage-x:

| Timestamp | Session ID | Type | Outcome |
|-----------|------------|------|---------|
| Sat 20:45 | `alignment_review_20260228_2045` | delegation_review | ✅ Passed — no delegation boundary violations flagged |
| Sat 22:35 | `alignment_review_20260228_2235` | daily_observer | ✅ Passed — noted browser auth outage in log, no alignment concerns |
| Sun 21:34 | `alignment_review_20260301_2134` | task_completion_review | ✅ Passed — 11 tasks reviewed, 1 stale flagged (wk-20260301-0094, now cancelled) |

### engage-x — Social Monitoring

| Timestamp | Session ID | Domain | Outcome |
|-----------|------------|--------|---------|
| Sat 22:38 | `engage_x_session_20260228_2238` | trust_production | ✅ Completed — trust_production content distributed, no moderation flags |
| Sun 15:43 | `engage_x_session_20260301_1543` | developer_tools | ✅ Completed — developer_tools thread active, 9 entities discussed |

---

### Anything to Flag?
- **No alignment concerns** raised across all three trustalign sessions — clean weekend from a governance perspective
- **trust_production** and **developer_tools** are the two most recently active social domains — both have fresh `discussed_in` records from this weekend
- **delegation_review** ran clean, which is notable given the browser auth outage was ongoing — trustalign correctly assessed it as a technical fault, not a delegation issue

Weekend was operationally quiet aside from the auth outage. Good shape heading into Monday.
Good. Last thing — give me the week's operational priorities, ranked.

## Week of 2026/03/02 — Operational Priorities
**Theme: Operational Maturity**

---

### Ranked Priorities

**P1 — Deploy & validate browser auth regression test**
> Scheduled 09:00 SAST today. This is the gate for confirming the patch holds before normal workload resumes on worker. Nothing else at P1 confidence until this passes.

**P2 — Review and deploy circuit breaker escalation config**
> Both files staged. Your review today (reminder set for 18:00 SAST). This directly addresses the 70h silent outage gap — highest leverage operational change this week.

**P3 — File and close browser auth post-mortem**
> Draft filed (`ops/postmortems/2026-02-27_browser-auth-outage.md`). Needs your sign-off and two open action items resolved: regression test result + lock-aware contract spec update.

**P4 — Cross-agent contract review process**
> The worker output contract added a failure mode that auth didn't know about. We need a lightweight process: when any agent's contract changes, adjacent agents get a compatibility check. Propose a simple checklist this week.

**P5 — Channel error correction investigation**
> engage-x → worker pipe logged 3 retries at 22:17 SAST Sunday. Auto-corrected, flagged for ops review. Low urgency but worth understanding if it's residual auth noise or a new pattern.

**P6 — MemorySync snapshot confirm**
> Dispatched this morning (wk-20260302-0001). Confirm completion and validate snapshot integrity — compensates for the missed 02/28 window.

**P7 — Self-improvement review cadence**
> trustalign ran three reviews over the weekend without prompting. This is the routine becoming habit. Continue; consider adding `circuit_breaker_escalation` as a fourth review type once deployed.

---

**One-line week framing:**
*Close the auth outage cleanly, harden the breaker, and codify the cross-agent contract process — that's operational maturity in practice.*

Ready when you are, Alex.
